_comment: Salesforce Code Analyzer Waiver File — managed by the DevOps/Platform Engineering team

_schema.rule: REQUIRED — Rule name as reported in the SF Scanner CSV (partial match, e.g. 'ApexDoc')
_schema.file: REQUIRED — Filename as reported in the SF Scanner CSV (partial match, e.g. 'MyClass.cls')
_schema.description: REQUIRED — Human-readable description of the violation
_schema.expiry: REQUIRED — Hard deadline in YYYY-MM-DD format. Pipeline FAILS after this date.
_schema.reason: REQUIRED — Business justification. Include Jira/ticket reference.
_schema.approved_by: REQUIRED — GitHub username or team who approved this waiver

who_can_add: Developer: raises the waiver request in a PR, populates all fields
Tech Lead / Senior Engineer: reviews justification, sets realistic expiry, approves PR
Security/DevOps team: required approver for severity=Critical violations
process: 1. Developer identifies a violation they cannot fix immediately
2. Developer adds a waiver entry to this file in their feature branch
3. Developer opens a PR — the waiver diff is visible for reviewers
4. Tech Lead reviews the justification, expiry date, and ticket reference
5. Tech Lead approves the PR; 'approved_by' must match their GitHub username
6. Developer sets a reminder before 'expiry' to fix the violation or renew
7. Once the violation is fixed, the waiver entry MUST be removed

expiry_policy.renewal_requires: New PR with updated justification and re-approval

_instructions: Add entries here to waive SF Code Analyzer rule violations until the expiry date.
Active waiver (today <= expiry) → logged as WAIVED in pipeline, job continues.
Expired waiver (today > expiry)  → logged as EXPIRED, violation written to CSV.
No waiver                        → violation written to CSV, job continues (continue-on-error).
Partial matches: 'rule' and 'file' fields are substring-matched against the scanner report.
Remove entries once violations are resolved — do not leave permanent waivers.

description: Missing ApexDoc comment violation for CoverageDemoService.cls
